Scenario.Description: Check whether LibreOffice is running and list open documents. Sets context for subsequent scenarios.

1. Call LibreOfficeWin LISTDOCS to enumerate open documents. If the UNO bridge is not reachable, LISTDOCS returns an error — call RELAUNCH. 2. Find the document window via KeyWin LISTWINDOWS (process="soffice.bin", title contains "LibreOffice"). 3. Record HANDLE:<hwnd> of the target window as {{hwnd}}.

Step.note: list open LO docs; also works with target=calc or impress

Scenario.Description: Open a blank LibreOffice Writer document.

Calls LibreOfficeWin NEWDOC with target=writer. After success, a new SALFRAME window with title "Untitled N - LibreOffice Writer" appears. Use KeyWin LISTWINDOWS → pick the window with title containing "LibreOffice Writer" → record HANDLE as {{hwnd}} for subsequent SENDKEYS operations.

Step.note: open blank Writer document; returns {success:true, name:'Untitled N - LibreOffice Writer'}

Scenario.Description: Open a blank LibreOffice Calc spreadsheet.
Calls LibreOfficeWin NEWDOC with target=calc. After success, a new SALFRAME window titled "Untitled N - LibreOffice Calc" appears.
Open a blank LibreOffice Impress presentation.

Calls LibreOfficeWin NEWDOC with target=impress.
KNOWN SIDE-EFFECT — TEMPLATE CHOOSER DIALOG: LibreOffice may show a "Select Template" / "New Presentation" modal dialog (SALSUBFRAME class, no WS_MAXIMIZEBOX) before the blank slide is ready. LibreOfficeWin.exe automatically attempts to dismiss it (WM_CLOSE + VK_ESC).
If NEWDOC returns success but you still see an unexpected SALSUBFRAME window in KeyWin LISTWINDOWS (title NOT containing "LibreOffice", "Impress", "Calc", "Writer", or "OpenOffice"), that is the template chooser. Dismiss it using KeyWin.exe: SENDKEYS {ESC} on HANDLE:<hwnd> of the dialog — cancels → blank presentation SENDKEYS {ENTER} on HANDLE:<hwnd> of the dialog — accepts highlighted template
This dialog handling MUST use KeyWin.exe (not LibreOfficeWin.exe), because: 1. It is a OS-level window, not a document element accessible via UNO. 2. KeyWin.exe can address any HANDLE: regardless of application. 3. LibreOfficeWin.exe has no command to interact with dialogs it did not create.
After the dialog is dismissed, the Impress document window (SALFRAME + WS_MAXIMIZEBOX, title "Untitled N - LibreOffice Impress") becomes the active document. Use KeyWin LISTWINDOWS → HANDLE of that window → assign to {{hwnd}}.

Step.note: open blank Impress presentation; LibreOfficeWin auto-dismisses template dialog

Scenario.Description: Close an unexpected modal dialog that is preventing further interaction. Uses KeyWin.exe to send ESC to the dialog window.

Use this scenario when KeyWin LISTWINDOWS reveals a SALSUBFRAME / dialog window belonging to soffice.bin that is NOT the main document window (i.e. has no WS_MAXIMIZEBOX, title does not contain app name keywords).
Identification steps: 1. KeyWin LISTWINDOWS target=SYSTEM 2. Find entry where process="soffice.bin" or "soffice.exe" AND title does NOT contain "LibreOffice" / "Writer" / "Calc" / "Impress" / "OpenOffice". 3. That HANDLE:<hwnd> is the dialog — assign to {{hwnd}}.
Dismissal: ESC → cancels / closes most dialogs without saving ENTER → confirms default button (accepts template, saves, etc.)
After dismissal, the dialog disappears and the document window is active. Always verify via LISTWINDOWS that no more dialogs remain.

Step.note: dismiss dialog — cancel / close without action

Scenario.Description: Calls LibreOfficeWin SAVE with target={{apptype}} (writer / calc / impress). If the document was never saved (new/unsaved), SAVE may trigger a Save As dialog (SALSUBFRAME — see scenario id="save-as-dialog"). PDF export: use parameter="pdf" or parameter="/full/path/to/file.pdf".

When a Save As dialog appears (SALSUBFRAME, title contains "Save As" or locale variant): 1. KeyWin LISTWINDOWS → find SALSUBFRAME with "Save" in title → record HANDLE as {{hwnd}}. 2. Type the destination path: KeyWin SENDKEYS "{{filepath}}" on HANDLE:{{hwnd}}. 3. Confirm: KeyWin SENDKEYS {ENTER} on HANDLE:{{hwnd}}. The dialog closes and the document is saved at {{filepath}}.

Step.note: type save path into file dialog

Scenario.Description: Sends Alt+F4 to close the target window (requires {{hwnd}} from KeyWin LISTWINDOWS). If the document has unsaved changes, LO shows a "Save?" dialog (SALSUBFRAME). To discard changes: send {TAB} to select "Discard / Don't Save" then {ENTER}, or reliably send {ALT+D} (Don't Save accelerator, EN locale) or {ESC} to cancel closing. Prefer saving first (scenario id="save-document") to avoid this dialog.
Hard-terminates all soffice processes. Use ONLY as a test teardown action. Unsaved changes will be lost. After this, LibreOfficeWin.exe must reconnect (call {RELAUNCH} if UNO socket mode was active).
